$comment: Curated, offline explanations of what writes to well-known Windows locations. Every entry is hand-written and cites primary documentation. There is deliberately no model-generated text and no confidence score here: an entry either exists or it does not.

entries.title: User temporary files
entries.writtenBy: Any program you run, plus Windows installers.
entries.ifRemoved: Programs recreate whatever they need. Files currently open are locked and will be skipped.

entries.title: System temporary files
entries.writtenBy: Windows itself and installers running as the system account.
entries.ifRemoved: Windows recreates the folder contents. Files held open by a running service are skipped.
entries.sourceTitle: Disk Cleanup in Windows - Microsoft Support

entries.title: Component store (WinSxS)
entries.writtenBy: Windows servicing. It holds every component version Windows can roll back to.
entries.ifRemoved: Do not delete this. Windows Update and feature installation break, and repair becomes impossible. Its reported size is also misleading: much of it is hard links to files that live elsewhere in Windows. Use DISM if you need to reclaim space.
entries.sourceTitle: Clean up the WinSxS folder - Microsoft Learn

entries.title: Windows system files
entries.writtenBy: Windows setup and Windows Update. It holds the operating system's own executables, drivers, and libraries.
entries.ifRemoved: Windows will not start. Nothing in this folder is safe to remove, and Stora refuses to modify it regardless of what any tool claims.

entries.title: Windows Installer cache
entries.writtenBy: Windows Installer, when MSI-based software is installed.
entries.ifRemoved: Do not delete this. Removing it breaks repair, update, and uninstall for every program installed through an MSI, often without warning until you try.
entries.sourceTitle: Do not delete the Installer folder - Microsoft Support

entries.title: Virtual memory paging file
entries.ifRemoved: Windows manages this file and will not allow it to be deleted while in use. Change its size through System Properties rather than removing it.
entries.sourceTitle: How to determine the appropriate page file size - Microsoft Learn

entries.title: Hibernation file
entries.writtenBy: Windows, to store memory contents when the machine hibernates.
entries.ifRemoved: It cannot be deleted directly. Turning hibernation off with `powercfg /hibernate off` removes it, but that also disables Fast Startup.

entries.title: Thumbnail and icon cache
entries.writtenBy: File Explorer, as you browse folders containing images and videos.
entries.ifRemoved: Explorer rebuilds it. Folders take slightly longer to display the first time you open them afterwards.
entries.sourceTitle: Disk Cleanup in Windows - Microsoft Support

entries.title: DirectX shader cache
entries.writtenBy: DirectX, when a game or application compiles graphics shaders.
entries.ifRemoved: Shaders are recompiled on demand. Expect brief stutter the first time a scene loads afterwards.
entries.sourceTitle: Disk Cleanup in Windows - Microsoft Support

entries.title: Windows Update downloads
entries.writtenBy: Windows Update, while staging update packages.
entries.ifRemoved: Windows downloads anything it still needs again. Do not remove this while an update is mid-installation.

entries.title: System Volume Information
entries.writtenBy: Windows, for System Restore points, indexing, and volume metadata.
entries.ifRemoved: Access is denied by design and Stora will not modify it. Manage restore point space through System Protection instead.

entries.title: Recycle Bin storage
entries.writtenBy: Windows, when you delete a file.
entries.ifRemoved: Emptying it is permanent. Use the Cleanup page or Explorer rather than deleting this folder directly.

entries.title: Installed npm packages
entries.writtenBy: npm, pnpm, or yarn, when a JavaScript project installs its dependencies.
entries.ifRemoved: Reinstalling restores it, but that needs network access and the registry has to still carry those versions. Safe to remove for a project you are not working on.

entries.title: Cargo build output
entries.writtenBy: Cargo, when a Rust project is built.
entries.ifRemoved: The next build recreates it, which takes as long as a clean build. `cargo clean` does the same thing.
entries.sourceTitle: Cargo Build Cache - The Cargo Book

entries.title: Unity asset database
entries.writtenBy: The Unity Editor, when it imports a project's assets.
entries.ifRemoved: Unity rebuilds it the next time the project opens. For a large project that reimport can take a very long time.

entries.title: WSL virtual disk
entries.writtenBy: Windows Subsystem for Linux. This one file holds an entire Linux filesystem.
entries.ifRemoved: Deleting it destroys that distribution and everything in it. It also does not shrink when files inside are deleted — compact it instead with `Optimize-VHD` after `wsl --shutdown`.
entries.sourceTitle: Manage disk space in WSL 2 - Microsoft Learn

entries.title: Prefetch data
entries.writtenBy: Windows, to speed up the programs you start most often.
entries.ifRemoved: Windows rebuilds it, but the programs it covers start more slowly until it does. There is little to gain: it is capped at 1024 entries and stays small.
